title: Powers – Part 38-04-04f
text:
Tax and Duty Manual Powers Manual Part 38-04-04f
Section 902 Taxes Consolidation Act 1997
Request of an authorised officer:
Information to be furnished by a third party
This document should be read in conjunction with section 902 Taxes Consolidation
Act 1997
Document reviewed November 2024
The information in this document is provided as a guide only and
is not professional advice, including legal advice. It should not be
assumed that the guidance is comprehensive or that it provides a
definitive answer in every case.
1

2

Tax and Duty Manual Powers Manual Part 38-04-04f
1 Introduction
Sections 902, 902A and 907A Taxes Consolidation Act 1997 (TCA) confer powers on
an authorised officer to require a third party other than a financial institution to
deliver books, records and other documents and to provide information in relation
to a taxpayer in certain circumstances.
The power to give notice directly to a third party under section 902 TCA requires the
consent of a Principal Officer/Branch Manager.
The powers to obtain
(a) a High Court Order under section 902A TCA, and
(b) the consent of the Appeal Commissioners under section 907A TCA
may be exercised with the consent of a Revenue Commissioner.
This manual deals with the powers conferred by section 902 TCA.
The Tax and Duty Manual on section 902A is Tax and Duty Manual Part 38-04-04g.
The Tax and Duty Manual on section 907A is Tax and Duty Manual Part 38-04-04h.
2 Notes on section 902 TCA
2.1 Definitions
The terms “books, records or other documents” and “liability” in relation to a person
rely on the definition of those terms in section 900(1) TCA.
The term “taxpayer” is defined to include a person or group, or class of persons not
known to the officer.
2.2 Serving of notice on third party
Section 902(2) TCA provides that notwithstanding:
 any obligation as to secrecy, or
 other restriction upon disclosure of information imposed by or under statute
or otherwise,
an authorised officer may, for the purpose of enquiring into a liability in relation to a
taxpayer, serve on another person (other than a financial institution) a notice in
writing requiring that person to do certain things with a specified time.
2.3 Precondition
Section 902(3) TCA provides that the authorised officer must have reasonable
grounds to believe that the third party is likely to have information relevant to the
establishment of a liability in relation to the taxpayer, before serving a notice under
this section.
3

Tax and Duty Manual Powers Manual Part 38-04-04f
2.4 Content of notice
The notice should contain the name of the person whose information the third party
is being asked to provide. The third party being asked to provide the information
may be a person connected with the taxpayer; this is implied by the generality of
section 902(2) TCA. Care should be taken that the notice only contains sufficient
information to enable the third party to comply with the notice and no unnecessary
additional information.
The notice may require the third party to do either or both of the following:
(a) to deliver or make available for inspection by the authorised officer such
book, records or other documents as are in the other person’s power,
possession or procurement and as contain or may contain information
relevant to a liability in relation to the taxpayer,
(b) to furnish to the authorised officer such information explanations and
particulars as the office may reasonably require and as are relevant to any
such liability,
and this must be specified in the notice. (See Appendix 1.) This is provided for in
section 902(2) TCA.
As noted in paragraph 2.1 above, a notice can be issued where the name of the
taxpayer is not known and can include a group or class of persons whose individual
identities are not known to the authorised officer.
2.5 Meaning of taxpayer
As well as a person or persons not known to the authorised officer, section 902(4)
TCA provides that the persons who may be treated as a taxpayer for the purposes of
this section include a company which has been dissolved or an individual who has
died.
2.6 Taxpayer to be advised of the service of the notice
Where a notice under section 902(2) TCA is served on a third party, the taxpayer
shall be advised of the service of the notice and upon whom it is served as soon as is
practicable thereafter. Section 902(6) TCA provides that where the identity of the
taxpayer is not known at the time of service of the notice, the taxpayer shall be
advised of the service of the notice and upon whom it is served as soon as is
practicable after the taxpayer’s identity becomes known to the authorised officer.
(See Appendix 2.)
2.7 Access by third party to records
Section 902(7) TCA states the third party must be allowed to inspect and copy any
books, records, or other documents which they have delivered, and which are
retained by the authorised officer.
4

Tax and Duty Manual Powers Manual Part 38-04-04f
2.8 Authorised officer may make extracts or copies
Section 902(8) TCA allows the authorised officer to make extracts from or copies of
documents made available.
2.9 Professional privilege
Section 902(9) TCA provides that no person is obliged to disclose:
a) information with respect to which a claim to legal professional privilege could
be maintained in legal proceedings;
b) information of a confidential medical nature; or
c) professional advice given to a client (other than such advice given as part of a
dishonest, fraudulent, or criminal purpose).
2.10 Reasonable assistance
Section 902(10) TCA requires a person who, in compliance with a notice under this
section makes books, records or other documents available for inspection by an
authorised officer to afford the officer reasonable assistance including information in
relation to the use of all electronic or other automatic means by which the books
etc. in non-legible form are capable of being reproduced in legible form.
2.11 Penalty
A penalty of €4,000 is provided in section 902(11) TCA for the refusal or failure by a
person to comply with a notice under this section or for failure to give the assistance
provided for in this section. Section 1078 TCA (Criminal Offences) is not to be treated
as applying to such failure or refusal.
3 Guidelines for authorised officers
3.1 Informing the taxpayer
 The taxpayer should first be given the opportunity either to supply the
information required (this may be possible by the taxpayer obtaining the
information, etc., directly from the third party) or to provide a mandate
authorising the officer to obtain the information from the third party – unless
the officer has good reasons to believe that this may defeat the purpose of
the enquiry.
 The taxpayer should, if appropriate, be informed briefly by reference to the
facts why the information is being sought.
 When the taxpayer is given the opportunity to supply the information, they
should be advised of the authorised officer’s power to serve a notice on the
third party.
 If the taxpayer refuses to co-operate and it would not defeat the purpose of
the enquiry to do so, a letter should be sent to the taxpayer advising them of
the intention to seek, ten days after the date of the letter, consent for the
5

Tax and Duty Manual Powers Manual Part 38-04-04f
issue of a notice under section 902 TCA and referring to the reasons for
taking such action.
 If the taxpayer makes representations within the ten-day period as to why a
section 902 TCA notice should not be issued, such representations should be
considered fully on their merits.
 If the officer believes that advance notice to the taxpayer would defeat the
purpose of the enquiry, the officer should proceed as outlined at paragraph
3.2 below, and include reasons for believing that advance notice would
defeat the purpose of the enquiry.
3.2 Preparation of report
Where no representations are received from the taxpayer within ten days, or where
any representations made are not accepted, or where the officer believes advance
notice to the taxpayer would defeat the purpose of the enquiry, the authorised
officer should send a report of the case to their Principal Officer/Branch Manager,
provided they have reasonable grounds to believe:
 that the third party is likely to have information, and
 that the information is relevant to the establishment of a liability in relation
to the taxpayer and which has not been disclosed to the officer’s satisfaction.
3.3 Content of report
The officer should clearly state their reasons why they believe the criteria set out in
paragraph 3.2 are satisfied.
The officer should specify the books, records or other documents of the third party
which they wish to inspect in relation to the taxpayer and/or the information
explanations or particulars which they wish the third party to supply and which are
relevant to the liability of the taxpayer. This can be a general description as long as it
is clear to the third party what is being asked for and in respect of what period.
The officer should complete a draft of the Form of Notice intended for issue to the
third party.
The information sought should only relate to the period under enquiry.
The report should set out all relevant facts on a numbered step by step progression
including details of any representations made as previously outlined.
All relevant papers should accompany the submission (tagged as appropriate for
ease of reference).
3.4 Time limit
A period of not less than 30 days that is, 30 days or more from the date of service of
the notice, must be allowed for compliance with the notice. This must be shown on
the notice. A period of thirty days is generally adequate.
6
